blockingIssues.description: resolve_spike maps SpikeNotFound to a successful CallToolResult containing "ERROR:" text instead of returning an MCP JSON-RPC error, violating VAL-MCP-005.
delete_spike maps SpikeNotFound to a successful CallToolResult containing "ERROR:" text instead of returning an MCP JSON-RPC error, violating VAL-MCP-007.
fetch_remote_spikes parses [] or { spikes: [...] }, but hosted API returns paginated { data: [...], next_cursor }, causing remote read tools to miss data.
Remote resolve_spike/delete_spike use PATCH/DELETE /spikes/:id, but worker currently exposes only GET /spikes/:id for spike-by-id.
submit_spike_remote can omit both project/projectKey when project_key arg is absent, but worker schema requires at least one of these fields.

nonBlockingIssues.description: Write-tool mutation tests are mostly serialization/helper checks and do not directly exercise resolve/delete success+error storage paths.
HTTP tools/list integration test can pass when tools payload is absent because assertions are conditionally skipped.

appliedUpdates.description: Added MCP completeness scrutiny notes to .factory/library/architecture.md covering hosted /spikes response shape, missing PATCH/DELETE spike routes, required project/projectKey in spike schema, and rmcp Streamable HTTP handshake testing details.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: For any CLI feature that integrates with --remote or hosted APIs, require explicit contract verification against ../spikes-hosted/worker routes and response schemas before implementation and in tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: mcp-remote-mode assumed nonexistent mutation routes and wrong GET /spikes response shape, resulting in three blocking contract mismatches.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit MCP error semantics guidance: contract-required error cases must return MCP JSON-RPC errors, not successful tool results with embedded error text.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: mcp-write-tools returned success payloads for not-found resolve/delete paths, violating VAL-MCP-005 and VAL-MCP-007.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Remove or correct stale instruction claiming commands/mcp.rs should be replaced entirely due to syntax errors, and tighten procedure adherence checks around TDD reporting.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Recent successful features incrementally modified mcp.rs; reviewer found skill text stale and adherence signaling inconsistent with transcript order.

rejectedObservations.observation: Single-session note about missing explicit cargo build command in one worker transcript.
General statement that mission docs should enforce TDD ordering from transcript evidence alone.
